ddls:
CREATE TABLE "safety_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the data entry */,
  "main" REAL /* Main parameter value */,
  "divg" REAL /* Divergence parameter value */,
  "mass" REAL /* Mass measurement */,
  "velo" REAL /* Velocity measurement */,
  "pres" REAL /* Pressure measurement */,
  "wall" REAL /* Wall integrity measurement */,
  "dump" REAL /* Dumping parameter value */,
  "part" REAL /* Particle parameter value */,
  "radi" REAL /* Radiation level */,
  "fire" REAL /* Fire safety parameter */,
  "comm" REAL /* Communication status */,
  "evac" REAL /* Evacuation status */,
  "hvac" REAL /* HVAC system status */,
  "total_t_used" REAL /* Total time used in seconds */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where data was collected */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment used for data collection */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used for data collection */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_safety_data_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment" (
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of equipment */,
  "equipment_name" TEXT /* Name of the equipment */,
  "equipment_type" TEXT /* Type of equipment (e.g., fire alarm, HVAC system) */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the equipment was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the equipment (e.g., operational, under maintenance) */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of last calibration */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for next maintenance */,
  "maintenance_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of maintenance required */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated data entry */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., fire risk, equipment failure) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Description of the alert message */,
  "alert_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES safety_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., safety officer, admin) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment being maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of maintenance activity */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance (e.g., completed, pending) */,
  "completed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who completed the maintenance */,
  "next_scheduled_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of next scheduled maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_completed_by FOREIGN KEY ("completed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "compliance_status" TEXT /* Status of compliance (e.g., compliant, non-compliant) */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_url" TEXT /* URL to access the full report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  "submission_status" TEXT /* Status of report submission (e.g., submitted, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_calibration" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calibration record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor being calibrated */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of calibration */,
  "calibration_value" REAL /* Calibration value */,
  "calibrated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the calibration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_calibration_calibrated_by FOREIGN KEY ("calibrated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_calibration_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, pressure) */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sensor (e.g., operational, faulty) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., upper, lower) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id")
)
